message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The government of India implemented social distancing interventions to contain the COVID-19 epidemic. However, effects on epidemic dynamics are yet to be understood.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Rates of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19 infections per day and effective reproduction number (
                    <jats:italic>
                      R
                      <jats:sub>t</jats:sub>
                    </jats:italic>
                    ) were estimated for 4 periods (Pre-lockdown and Lockdown Phases 1 to 3) according to nationally implemented phased interventions. Adoption of these interventions was estimated using Google mobility data. Estimates at the national level and for 12 Indian states most affected by COVID-19 are presented.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Findings</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Daily case rates ranged from 0·03 to 30·05/10 million people across 4 discrete periods in India. From May 4-17, 2020, the National Capital Territory (NCT) of Delhi had the highest case rate (222/10 million people/day), whereas Kerala had the lowest (2·18/10 million/day). Average
                    <jats:italic>
                      R
                      <jats:sub>t</jats:sub>
                    </jats:italic>
                    was 1·99 (95% CI 1·93-2·06) for India; it ranged from 1·38 to 2·78, decreasing over time. Median mobility in India decreased in all contact domains, with the lowest being 21% in retail/recreation (95% CI 13-46%), except home which increased to 129% (95% CI 117-132%) compared to the 100% baseline value.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Interpretation</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The Indian government imposed strict contact mitigation, followed by a phased relaxation, which slowed the spread of COVID-19 epidemic progression in India. The identified daily COVID-19 case rates and
                    <jats:italic>
                      R
                      <jats:sub>t</jats:sub>
                    </jats:italic>
                    will aid national and state governments in formulating ongoing COVID-19 containment plans. Furthermore, these findings may inform COVID-19 public health policy in developing countries with similar settings to India.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Funding</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Non-funded.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Public health interventions in India slowed the spread of COVID-19 epidemic dynamics

reference.article-title: How will country-based mitigation measures influence the course of the COVID-19 epidemic?
